questions.question: What is a 'Memory Leak' in sales?

options.text: Forgetting your login password
Losing critical deal details because you failed to document them immediately
The CRM crashing
Forgetting the prospect's name during the call

questions.explanation: Human memory is volatile. If you don't write down the specifics (Metrics, Timeline, Names) immediately, you lose them, which damages your credibility later.

questions.question: Why is recording 'Verbatim Quotes' powerful?

options.text: It fills up space in the CRM
It allows you to use their exact words back to them later, maximizing resonance and trust
It helps you learn slang
It's required by law

questions.explanation: If you say 'We improve efficiency', it sounds like marketing. If you say 'We fix the process that is bleeding you dry' (their words), it sounds like Empathy.

questions.question: When should you document your notes?

options.text: At the end of the week
Immediately after the call (The 5-minute block)
During the call only
Never

questions.explanation: Recall degrades exponentially. Waiting even an hour results in significant data loss.

questions.question: If you are handing the deal off to a technical co-founder for a demo, why is documentation critical?

options.text: So the co-founder doesn't have to ask the same discovery questions again (which annoys the prospect)
So the co-founder can charge more
So you can blame the co-founder if it fails
So the co-founder knows the weather

questions.explanation: Repeating questions makes a company look disorganized. Good documentation allows the second person to pick up exactly where the first person left off.

questions.question: What is the best format for discovery notes?

options.text: A long stream-of-consciousness paragraph
A structured Scorecard (BANT/MEDDIC) with specific fields
A sticky note on your monitor
Audio recording only

questions.explanation: Structure allows for quick scanning and scoring. 'BANT: Strong' is easier to parse than a 500-word paragraph.
